commands.review_summary:
The PR changes unified text provider catalog projection to skip unreadable provider entries and model rows while adding provider-entry tests that preserve healthy sibling rows.

PR surface: Source +68, Tests +82. Total +150 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: Do we have a high-confidence way to reproduce the issue? Yes from source: current main reads provider entries and model rows without guards, and the PR adds focused tests with throwing getters for both failure positions; I did not run tests in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Protected maintainer PR with provider-routing compatibility tradeoffs; the next action is explicit maintainer acceptance, not an automated repair.

target.title: fix(plugin-sdk): isolate provider catalog projection failures
target.body:
## Summary

- narrows provider catalog hardening to unified text-catalog projection only
- skips unreadable provider entries and unreadable model rows while preserving healthy sibling rows
- removes the broader shared safe-record helper/auth-slot swallowing from the prior branch shape
- uses only synthetic `fuzzplugin` / `mockplugin` fixtures in tests and PR notes

## Verification

- rebuilt the branch from current `origin/main` 4dad7bd93b6c and replaced the earlier oversized 6-file branch with a 2-file projection-only patch
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugin-sdk/provider-entry.test.ts`: 1 unit-fast shard, 5 tests passed on head c11f6d7f0e75
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/plugins/provider-catalog-unified-text.ts src/plugin-sdk/provider-entry.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/plugins/provider-catalog-unified-text.ts src/plugin-sdk/provider-entry.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- touched-file scrub for reported fixture names, private local paths, and install-error output: clean
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`: clean, no accepted/actionable findings
- AWS Crabbox `pnpm check:changed`: passed, run `run_785a0ba44673`, lease `cbx_6d3cc03a2f0d` / `silver-crayfish`, command 5m40s, total 7m55s; lease released after run

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: malformed provider catalog/model metadata no longer crashes unified text-catalog projection or hides healthy sibling provider entries.

Real environment tested: linked OpenClaw `gwt` worktree on current `origin/main` 4dad7bd93b6c with focused provider-entry tests on head c11f6d7f0e75; AWS Crabbox Linux changed gate on the same patch.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: rebuilt the branch from current main, pruned the previous shared-helper/auth hardening, ran focused provider-entry tests, formatter check, targeted lint check, diff check, touched-file scrub, autoreview, and remote `pnpm check:changed` via Crabbox.

Evidence after fix: tests prove unreadable provider catalog entries and unreadable catalog model rows are skipped while healthy sibling entries remain available in the unified text catalog; Crabbox changed gate passed the impacted core/extension lanes.

Observed result after fix: one malformed plugin-owned provider metadata row no longer poisons the whole unified provider catalog projection surface.

What was not tested: no full live provider setup flow. The patch is now scoped to projection behavior and covered by provider SDK unit tests plus the remote changed gate.
target.author: vincentkoc
